stories.title: The Dresser
stories.description:
Little Olive was sleeping in her cosy bed, dreaming about her castle and her mum who she loved more than anything in the world.

But suddenly something felt wrong.

Olive opened her eyes slowly. The room was dark except for the thin strip of light from the hallway. And there she was again. The old woman standing inside the open dresser, bent slightly to fit between the hanging coats. Her face was pale like paper and her eyes were wide and angry, staring directly at Olive.

Olive quietly slipped out of bed and walked to her bedroom door. She didn’t want to wake her mum again. Her mum had already checked the dresser three times this week and told her there was nothing there.

Olive looked back one last time.

The dresser door was closed now.

And the old woman was standing beside her bed.

stories.title: The Hallway
stories.description:
Tom woke up because he heard footsteps in the hallway.

Slow ones. Dragging slightly against the wooden floor.

He sat up and listened. His parents' room was at the end of the corridor, so it had to be one of them walking to the bathroom. But the steps didn’t sound right. They stopped outside his door.

The handle slowly began to turn.

Tom quickly closed his eyes and pretended to sleep. The door creaked open and something stepped into the room. He could hear the slow breathing, feel the weight of someone standing beside his bed.

The footsteps stayed there for a long time.

Much longer than a person should just stand and watch.

Then the voice of his mum suddenly called from the other end of the hallway.

“Tom? Who are you talking to?”

stories.title: Goodnight, Sleep Tight
stories.description:
Little James was being put to bed. He lay quietly under the blankets, his eyes wet with tears but not making a single sound.

His grandma tucked the blanket around him, smoothed his hair, and softly sang the lullaby she always used to sing. When she finished, she kissed his forehead, turned off the bedside lamp, and said gently, “Sleep well, my sweet boy. I’ll see you tomorrow.”

Then she stepped into the closet.

James held his breath for as long as he could. His chest burned, but he stayed perfectly still. Only after the closet door slowly creaked shut did he begin sobbing.

His mum rushed in and turned on the light.

“James, what's wrong?”

He pointed at the closet, still shaking.

“Grandma came to say goodnight again.”

His mum's face went pale.

“James… Grandma died a month ago.”

James wiped his eyes and whispered,

“I know”, he said

stories.title: The Mirror
stories.description:
Emily was brushing her hair in front of the mirror when she noticed something strange. The reflection didn't match her movements. When she lifted her hand, the reflection stayed still. When she turned her head, the reflection didn't follow.

Emily’s heart started pounding. She tried to look away, but the reflection kept staring at her with cold, empty eyes.

She wanted to scream, but no sound came out. The reflection began to smile, revealing sharp, jagged teeth.

Emily felt a cold breath on her neck and heard a whisper in her ear.

“Let me out.”

stories.title: The Phone Call
stories.description:
Mark woke up to the sound of his phone vibrating on the bedside table.

3:14 AM.

He squinted at the screen. The caller ID said Home.

That was strange. He was at home. His phone number shouldn't even be able to call itself.

He answered anyway.

For a few seconds there was only silence. Then he heard breathing.

Very quiet. Very close.

Then his own voice whispered through the speaker.

“Don't turn around.”

stories.title: The Window
stories.description:
Emily always liked sleeping with her curtains open so she could see the stars.

One night she woke up suddenly, feeling like someone was watching her. The room was quiet. The street outside was empty.

But something was wrong with the window.

Someone was standing outside it.

She lived on the third floor.

The figure didn't move. It just stared at her through the glass with a long, stretched smile.

Emily pulled the blanket over her head and stayed there until morning.

When she finally looked again, the figure was gone.

But the outside of the window was covered in fingerprints.

From the inside.

stories.title: The Wardrobe
stories.description:
Daniel hated the wardrobe in his new room.

It wasn't old or broken or anything like that. It was just a normal wardrobe.

Except every night, right after the lights were turned off, the door would slowly open by itself.

His parents said it was because the floor was uneven.

But Daniel noticed something they hadn't.

The gap inside the wardrobe was getting bigger each night.

Like someone inside was pushing it open a little more.

Last night the door opened wide enough for him to see the back wall.

It wasn't a wall.

It was another room.

And something inside it whispered his name.

stories.title: The Breathing
stories.description:
Ryan woke up in the middle of the night because he could hear breathing.

Slow. Heavy breathing.

At first he thought it was his own, but when he held his breath the sound continued.

He stayed completely still in the darkness, listening.

The breathing was coming from under the bed.

Then something gently knocked twice against the wooden slats beneath him.

Like it knew he was awake.

stories.title: The New House
stories.description:
After moving into the new house, Clara started noticing small things that didn't make sense.

The kitchen cupboards would be open in the morning. The hallway light would be on even though she was sure she turned it off before bed. Once, she even heard footsteps upstairs when she was alone.

Eventually she installed a few small cameras around the house.

The next day she watched the recordings.

At 2:13 AM every night, she calmly walks out of her bedroom, stands in the hallway for ten minutes, and slowly opens every cupboard.

Clara has lived alone for six years.

She also sleepwalks.

The strange part is the figure standing behind her in every recording, patiently waiting for her to finish.

stories.title: The Dog
stories.description:
Sam's dog started barking every night at exactly the same time.

3:02 AM.

It would sit at the bedroom door, growling softly at the dark hallway. Sam tried everything. Closing the door, turning on lights, even checking the entire house.

Nothing was ever there.

One night Sam finally opened the door and let the dog out.

The dog ran straight down the hall and stopped halfway, staring into the empty air.

Then it slowly backed away, whining.

Sam followed its gaze.

The hallway mirror was reflecting someone standing behind him.

stories.title: The Text Message
stories.description:
Evan received a message from an unknown number.

“Don't go home tonight.”

He laughed and ignored it.

A few minutes later another message arrived.

“I'm serious.”

Evan replied: Who is this?

A photo appeared instantly.

It was a picture of his house.

Taken from inside the living room.

stories.title: The Child
stories.description:
A little boy told his mother there was a man in his closet.

She checked, of course. Nothing inside except clothes and toys.

“See?” she said. “No one there.”

The boy nodded slowly.

“I know,” he whispered.

“He only comes out after you leave.”

stories.title: The Recording
stories.description:
Jake sometimes recorded himself sleeping to check if he was snoring.

On one of those occasions he watched the video back in the morning.

Everything looked normal for hours. Just him sleeping in the dark room.

Then at 4:26 AM his eyes suddenly opened.

He sat up slowly.

But he didn't move like someone waking up.

He moved like someone trying to remember how to use a body.

stories.title: The Apartment
stories.description:
When Nina moved into her apartment, the landlord warned her about the pipes.

“They make strange noises at night,” he said.

He was right.

Sometimes she heard faint knocking inside the walls.

One night the knocking started again, louder than usual.

Three knocks. Then three more.

Nina tapped the wall twice as a joke.

The knocking stopped.

Then it continued.

From inside the bedroom.

stories.title: The Security Camera
stories.description:
Paul checked the security camera app on his phone before going to sleep.

Everything looked normal. The front door, the empty hallway, the dark living room.

Just to be safe, he rewound the footage from earlier.

At first nothing happened.

Then he saw himself walk through the front door.

Paul frowned.

He had been home all evening.

The man in the video was still standing in the hallway, staring directly at the camera.

stories.title: The Museum
stories.description:
The museum guard liked the late shift because the building was completely empty.

Around midnight he walked the sculpture hall like always, checking the same statues he'd seen hundreds of times before. Marble faces staring blankly into the room.

Tonight one of them looked slightly different.

He stepped closer.

The statue in the corner had its head turned toward the door.

The plaque beneath it read:

“Untitled. Marble. 1873. If found facing the door, leave the building immediately.”

stories.title: The Train
stories.description:
A woman sat alone in the last carriage of the night train, scrolling through her phone.

At the next stop, a man stepped in and sat across from her. He looked pale and tired, like someone who hadn't slept in days.

He stared at her for a moment and asked quietly, “Excuse me… what year is it?”

She laughed nervously and told him.

The man slowly leaned back in his seat.

“Good,” he said. “Then the train hasn't crashed yet.”

stories.title: The Library
stories.description:
A student found a strange book in the back corner of the library.

The title was simply “You.”

Inside, the pages described someone's life in detail.

School. Friends. Childhood memories.

The student laughed nervously when the descriptions started sounding familiar.

They flipped to the last page.

The final sentence read:

The reader will finish this sentence in the library at 4:17 PM. Death will arrive a minute later.

The student checked their watch.

It was 4:16.

stories.title: The Train Car
stories.description:
A nearly empty train rolled through the countryside.

A passenger looked up from his book and noticed the man sitting across from him hadn't moved for a very long time.

Not blinking.

Not breathing.

Just staring.

Finally the passenger leaned forward and said quietly,

“Are you alright?”

The man slowly smiled.

“I was waiting to see if you could tell the difference.”

stories.title: The Elevator Mirror
stories.description:
A woman stepped into an elevator alone.

The mirrored walls reflected her from every angle.

The doors closed and the elevator began to move.

For a moment she stared at the reflections.

Then her stomach dropped.

One of them was smiling.

stories.title: The Forest Tower
stories.description:
A fire lookout climbed the long staircase to the forest watchtower at sunset.

When he reached the top, the door to the cabin was already open.

Inside, the logbook sat on the desk.

The last entry had been written just minutes earlier.

“Something is climbing the tower.”

stories.title: The Empty Train
stories.description:
A maintenance worker was checking an abandoned train that had been left overnight in the station.

Every carriage was empty.

Except one.

In the last car, a single suitcase sat neatly in the middle of the aisle.

When he opened it, he found dozens of photographs.

All of them were pictures taken inside that exact train.

Every photo showed empty seats.

Except the last one.

It showed him opening the suitcase.

stories.title: The School Playground
stories.description:
During recess, a teacher noticed one child standing completely still near the fence.

“Why aren't you playing with the others?” she asked gently.

The little boy pointed toward the woods behind the playground.

“I'm waiting for the man to come back.”

“What man?”

“The one who came out of the ground.”

stories.title: The Elevator
stories.description:
A janitor stepped into an empty elevator late at night.

Just before the doors closed, a little girl ran in and stood beside him.

She pressed the button for the basement.

The janitor smiled politely.

“I didn't know children lived in the building.”

The girl looked confused.

“You can see me?”

stories.title: The Park Lake
stories.description:
A woman watched her daughter throwing bread to the ducks.

The girl suddenly stopped and stared into the water.

“What do you see?” her mum asked.

The girl pointed at the dark reflection beneath the surface.

“The child”

Her mum laughed.

“There is no child, sweetheart”

The girl shook her head.

“The child is under the water looking up.”

stories.title: The Babysitter
stories.description:
The babysitter was washing dishes when she heard a small voice behind her.

“Can you help me reach the cookies?”

She turned and saw a little boy standing in the kitchen doorway.

“Oh! You must be one of the kids! I thought everyone was asleep” she said. “What's your name?”

“Daniel” the boy replied quietly.

Later, when the parents came home, the babysitter mentioned him.

The parents stared at her for a long moment.

The mother swallowed.

“Daniel was our first son.”

“He died in this house eight years ago.”

stories.title: The Night Visitor
stories.description:
A mother woke to find her daughter standing beside the bed.

“Mum,” she whispered.

“I had a bad dream.”

The mother sighed and sat up.

“What was it about?”

The girl looked confused.

“You.”

“Me?”

The girl nodded slowly.

“You were crying because you couldn't find my body.”

stories.title: The Hospital Room
stories.description:
A nurse walked past a quiet hospital room late at night.

A small boy sat on the bed swinging his legs.

“Where are your parents?” the nurse asked.

“They went home.”

The nurse checked the chart on the door.

The room had been empty all evening.

She looked back inside.

The bed was empty.

But the blanket was still moving.

stories.title: The Schoolyard
stories.description:
A teacher noticed a child sitting alone on the swings after school.

“All the other children went home,” she said gently.

The child kept swinging slowly.

“I can't.”

“Why not?”

“My bus already left.”

The teacher smiled. “We'll call your parents.”

The child shook his head.

“They will just be upset... they can't see me, after all”

stories.title: The Lake
stories.description:
A boy was throwing stones into a lake when another child sat beside him.

“Do you want to see something?” the second boy asked.

“What?”

“The place where I fell in.”

The first boy looked at the water.

“How long ago?”

The boy thought for a moment.

“20 years ago, but my mum still cries about it.”

stories.title: The Old Toy
stories.description:
A mother was cleaning her daughter's room when she noticed an old doll sitting on the bed.

“I thought we threw this away,” she said.

Her daughter looked up from the floor.

“We did.”

“Then how did it get back?”

Her daughter shrugged.

“She brought it.”

“Who?”

“The girl who wants her room back.”
